warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for furan were computed with density‐functional theory at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier “standard”) in PySCF (no density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.  The calculation was a set of single-point SCF evaluations on the user-supplied furan geometry (no re-optimization).  SCF cycles for the neutral (N), cation (N-1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states all converged, and the integrity monitor reports the run as trustworthy.

Headline result (electrophilic Fukui function f⁺): the largest f⁺ value is 1.9242931 on atom 5 (index 4, C).  The same carbon is also the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.1616371.  Full per-atom descriptors:
  Atom (index 0-based):   f⁺          f⁻          f⁰ (dual)
  0 O 0 :  −1.3316182   0.08382155  −0.62389834
  1 C 1 :   1.2286866   0.08910309   0.65889485
  2 C 2 :   1.2270264   0.08930482   0.65816562
  3 C 3 :   1.9233053   0.16152700   1.0424161
  4 C 4 :   1.9242931   0.16163710   1.0429651  ← most electro-/nucleophilic
  5 H 5 :  −1.2190639   0.10248412  −0.55828991
  6 H 6 :  −1.2191056   0.10251350  −0.55829603
  7 H 7 :  −0.76678308  0.10481615  −0.33098346
  8 H 8 :  −0.76674059  0.10479266  −0.33097397

The live PySCF output was streamed to the log file produced by the skill run; all integrity checks passed.  No engine warnings were reported beyond the standard charge-conservation notices echoed below.
